title: HTTPS Broken: Certificate Expired
description:
## The Situation

Users are getting `ERR_CERT_DATE_INVALID` in their browsers. Your site's SSL certificate expired 3 days ago.

A new certificate has already been issued and is sitting in `/etc/ssl/renewed/`. You need to update the nginx configuration to use it and reload nginx.

## Your Task

1. Identify which certificate nginx is currently using
2. Confirm the new certificate is valid
3. Update the nginx config to point to the new certificate
4. Reload nginx

## Useful Commands

```bash
# Check current certificate expiry
openssl x509 -in /etc/ssl/certs/app.crt -noout -dates

# Check the new certificate
openssl x509 -in /etc/ssl/renewed/app.crt -noout -dates

# View nginx SSL config
cat /etc/nginx/sites-available/app

# Test and reload nginx
nginx -t && nginx -s reload

# Verify the running cert
echo | openssl s_client -connect localhost:443 2>/dev/null | openssl x509 -noout -dates
```
hints: Check both certificates with openssl x509 -noout -dates to see which is expired and which is valid.
The new certificate is in /etc/ssl/renewed/. The nginx config still points to /etc/ssl/certs/. Update the ssl_certificate and ssl_certificate_key paths.
Edit /etc/nginx/sites-available/app. Change ssl_certificate to /etc/ssl/renewed/app.crt and ssl_certificate_key to /etc/ssl/renewed/app.key. Then run: nginx -t && nginx -s reload
